# React Doctor

Diagnose React codebase health. Scans for security, performance, correctness, and architecture issues, then outputs a 0–100 score with actionable diagnostics.

Note: dead-code detection was removed in v0.2 (previously powered by knip). For dead-code analysis, run `npx knip` directly.

## Usage

Run this at your project root:

```bash
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned
```

Use `--verbose` to see affected files and line numbers:

```bash
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned --verbose
```

Use `--scope changed` to report only the issues a change introduced vs a base branch (set the base with `--base <ref>`; auto-detected `main`/`master` when omitted). On the default branch, scans uncommitted working-tree changes; on a feature branch, scans changes vs the base.

```bash
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned --verbose --scope changed
```

Use `--score` to output only the numeric score:

```bash
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned --score
```

Use `--json` for a single structured JSON report (suppresses other output):

```bash
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned --json | jq '.summary'
```

Use `-y` to skip prompts (required for non-interactive environments like CI or coding agents):

```bash
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned -y
```

Use `--staged` for pre-commit hooks (scans only staged files):

```bash
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned --staged
```

react-doctor blocks (exits non-zero) when an error-severity diagnostic is found, so CI fails by default. Use `--blocking <level>` to change the threshold — `error` (default), `warning` (fail on any diagnostic), or `none` (advisory; findings and the score are still reported, but the process always exits 0):

```bash
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned --blocking none
```

Use `--no-score` to skip the score API and the share URL. Runs with scoring disabled print no score; the rest of the report renders normally:

```bash
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned --no-score
```

Install the skill into your coding agent (50+ agents supported via agent-install: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Factory Droid, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot, Goose, OpenCode, Pi, Windsurf, Roo Code, Cline, Kilo Code, Warp, Replit, OpenHands, Continue, and more):

```bash
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned install
```

## Configure rules

Configure with a `doctor.config.ts` (or `.js`, `.mjs`, `.cjs`, `.json`, `.jsonc`) in your project root, or a `"reactDoctor"` key in `package.json`. JSON configs allow comments and trailing commas; point `$schema` at `https://raw.githubusercontent.com/gcharang/react-doctor/pinned/packages/website/public/schema/config.json` for editor autocomplete.

Manage which rules run with the `rules` subcommands. They edit your `doctor.config.*` in place (TS/JS edits preserve formatting) — or `package.json#reactDoctor` — so you don't have to hand-write config:

```bash
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned rules list                 # every rule + the severity it runs at
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned rules list --configured     # only rules your config changed
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned rules explain <rule>        # why a rule matters + how to configure it
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned rules disable <rule>        # turn a rule off (rules.<key> = "off")
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned rules enable <rule>         # turn it back on at its recommended severity
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned rules set <rule> warn       # off | warn | error
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned rules category "React Native" off   # set a whole category
npx github:gcharang/react-doctor#pinned rules ignore-tag design     # skip a rule family (design, test-noise, …)
```

Rule references accept the full key (`react-doctor/no-danger`), the bare id (`no-danger`), or a legacy key (`react/no-danger`). `ignore.tags` disables matching rules before linting — a tagged rule stays off even with a `rules`/`categories` override; otherwise `rules` overrides `categories` overrides the rule's default. Use the `surfaces` config field (not `disable`) to keep a rule locally but hide it from PR comments, the score, or the CI gate.

## Options

```
Usage: react-doctor [directory] [options]

Options:
  -v, --version      display the version number
  --lint             enable linting (default: on)
  --no-lint          skip linting
  --verbose          show file details per rule
  --output-dir <dir>  directory for the full diagnostics dump (default: a temp folder)
  --score            output only the score
  --json             output a single structured JSON report (suppresses other output)
  -y, --yes          skip prompts, scan all workspace projects
  --project <name>   select projects: workspace names or directory paths (comma-separated for multiple); set `projects` in doctor.config.* to make the selection persistent (the flag wins)
  --scope <value>    how much to scan/report: full (default), files, changed (only new issues vs base), or lines
  --base <ref>       base git ref for files/changed/lines scope (auto-detected when omitted)
  --no-score         skip the score API and share URL
  --staged           scan only staged (git index) files for pre-commit hooks
  --blocking <level>  severity that fails CI: error (default), warning, none
  --debug            force a Sentry trace and print its id at the end (paste it into a bug report)
  -h, --help         display help for command
```
